successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.interface.ts exports ILeaveManagementService with getLeaveBalance and getLeaveHistory methods added.

src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts injects IAuditService and enforces RBAC: approve/reject only allowed for users with role 'manager' who are the manager of the requesting employee.
src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts enforces RBAC: cancel allowed for owning employee or their manager; discard draft allowed only for owning employee when status is 'draft'.
getLeaveBalance returns only the authenticated employee's balances when role is 'employee', and any employee's balances when role is 'manager' and the employee is in their team.
getLeaveHistory returns only the authenticated employee's requests when role is 'employee', and all team requests when role is 'manager' and the employee is the manager of the requesters.
All state-changing methods (approve, reject, cancel, submit) write an audit record via IAuditService.log with correct entity type, entity id, changed by, old and new values.
approveLeaveRequest and rejectLeaveRequest execute the status update and audit log insertion within a single database transaction to ensure atomicity.

Tests verify RBAC enforcement: a non-manager user calling approve/reject receives an error; a manager not managing the employee receives an error; an employee calling cancel on another's request receives an error.
Tests verify getLeaveBalance and getLeaveHistory return correct data scoped to the user's role.
Tests verify audit logging is called with correct parameters for each state-changing operation.
Test file exists only at src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts, no duplicate test file, and all tests pass.

constraints: All state-changing operations must write an audit record via IAuditService.log with correct entity type, entity id, changed by, old and new values.

RBAC enforcement: only managers can approve/reject, employees can only act on their own requests; cancel allowed for owning employee or their manager; discard draft allowed only for owning employee when status is 'draft'.
Approve and reject operations must execute status update and audit log within a single database transaction.
All database access must go through repository interfaces (ILeaveRepository, IBalanceRepository, etc.).
No sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, financial data) in logs.
All async errors must be caught and handled; no unhandled promise rejections.
Test file must be placed only at src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts; no duplicate test files.
outOfScope: Implementation of leave request lifecycle (submit, approve, reject) beyond RBAC and audit integration (assumed already exists from Phase 9).
Modifications to existing modules (employee, policy, balance, leave, notification) outside the leave-management service.
End-to-end or integration tests beyond unit tests for the service.
